spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (SharedWorker) with runtime script (~3415 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (4 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (counter, every, extendedlifetime, keeps, resets, second, standard, worker).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (2 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 40 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("This update adds a new option, `extendedLifetime: true`, to the `SharedWorker` constructor. This new option requests that the shared worker be kept alive even after all current clients have unloaded. This allows pages to perform asynchronou") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/commit/9c009049e4fa9dba638ef68ca502b781082bbb68 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3415 chars of script across 4 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 3 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
